entry.title: Re: Send email notification when an existing Termination...
entry.content: This cannot be achieved using EC Business Rules or standard workflows because termination date edits do not create an effective-dated change. The supported solution is to use Intelligent Services (Job Information Changed / Termination event) with a condition on termination date change, which will reliably trigger an email notification.

entry.title: Re: #CoE#QUSS How to delete user on CBC directly?
entry.content:
Dear @davidhe79,

No you can’t delete a user in S/4 directly from CBC in a 3SL Public Cloud landscape

What you can do depends on what you mean by user:

1. Remove access to CBC

In CBC you can remove the user from the project team / deactivate or terminate their CBC access so they can’t log on to CBC / work in CBC tasks. Go to Settings ->Team

2. Delete the identity SSO user

CBC does not own the identity. If you use SSO:

  • Delete/disable the user in IAS or in your corporate IdP if that’s the source of truth
  • And let IPS provisioning update downstream assignments

SAP Notes and SAP help below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0002651251

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003301639

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/user-management

3. Remove the business user in Public Cloud tenants (DEV/TEST/PROD)

If your goal is the user inside Public Cloud tenant:

  • Do it in each tenant (DEV/TEST/PROD) using the standard SAP user administration typically deactivate/lock and remove roles, deletion is often restricted/less common for audit reasons for example:
    • Maintain Business Users (F1303)

More details below:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F1303')/S36

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/e40e710321c74f28916affa9ae984bce.html

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: What's New on SAP Analytics Cloud Home Screen 2026 Q1 QRC: New Admin Cards
entry.content:
I'm excited to introduce three new Administrator Cards on the SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) Home screen in the Q1 QRC release. These cards give administrators a direct, customizable presence on users’ home screens and surface the operational insights needed to keep your SAC environment healthy and well governed with less time spent hunting for root causes.

What’s new

1 - Admin custom card

  • What it is: A fully customizable card that lets administrators display access links, announcements, and any other custom messages directly on users’ home screens.
  • How to create it: from the admin settings and under default appearance tab, click "+ Add Custom Card"

  •  What you can include: images, text, tags, and links tailored to the message or audience.

  • Important behavior: If an administrator locks and saves the custom card, users will not be able to move or hide it from their home screen.
  • Benefits: Deliver timely announcements (upgrades, maintenance windows, training links), promote key content or processes, and ensure critical guidance is seen by target audiences.

2- Administrative actions card

  • What it is: A focused card that summarizes critical recommendations for admins.
  • Purpose: Reduce time spent on root-cause analysis by surfacing key actionable items and prioritized recommendations.
  • Benefits: Enables faster, proactive housekeeping and maintenance helping admins resolve issues earlier and keep the system running smoothly.

 3 - System monitor card

  • What it is: A dedicated admin card that brings a concise, comprehensive summary of system health to the Home screen.
  • What it shows: critical system metrics, user/license health, performance hotspots, and governance-related recommendations.
  • Benefits: Empowers administrators to spot trends, identify performance issues quickly, and make informed decisions about scaling, licensing, or governance actions.

 How these cards help your organization

  • Improve visibility: Place critical notices and system health indicators where admins and users will see them daily.
  • Save time: The Administrative Actions card reduces investigation time by collecting high-priority recommendations in one place.
  • Encourage consistency: Locked custom cards ensure essential guidance (policies, access instructions, or emergency contacts) remains visible.
  • Drive governance: The System Monitor card makes it easier to track license usage, performance patterns, and governance recommendations without running separate reports.

Quick admin tips

  • Use images and tags on the custom card to increase clarity and engagement for announcements and links.
  • Lock and save a custom card only when you want fixed visibility for all users; otherwise let users personalize their Home experience.
  • Treat the Administrative Actions card as your operational to-do list, prioritize items and turn recurring recommendations into scheduled maintenance tasks.
  • Monitor the System Monitor card regularly to catch performance hotspots early and adjust configurations or scale resources as needed.

Getting started

These cards arrive with the Q1 QRC release. If you’re an admin, check your Home screen configuration options to enable and customize these cards. If you need to align messaging across teams, plan your card content and locking strategy before publishing.

entry.content:
We are trying to turn on "Lexicographical" indicator for Product masters. This allows creating product numbers with leading zeroes. However, it does not allow you to set this indicator if you have numeric materials already created in the tenant. In our case, we have a handful of numeric materials in both our Cust and Test tenant that is preventing us from carrying out this configuration. We tried archiving these materials but there are stocks for these materials from previous year that we are unable to remove due to the financial posting periods that cannot go back more than one period. Madhavan

entry.title: Re: Behind the Curtains - what's new about Maintenance Certificates
entry.content: @1jules There is no need to create a dedicated federation lead for every use case. The role of the federation lead is only important during federation setup. Once all SAP Host Agents belong to the same federation (which is a prerequisite for the Maintenance Certificate functionality), they would also be capable to gather HANA metrics of the ABAP Instances.  With other words, with the same federation lead both use cases are working fine.

entry.title: F-92 Full Asset Retirement Posting Both Gain and Loss
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am observing unusual posting behavior during F-92 (Asset Retirement with Customer) and need confirmation if this is standard SAP behavior.

Scenario:

  • Full retirement of asset

  • Sales value: PKR 494,829

  • No taxes involved

  • Asset has multiple depreciation areas (Book + Revaluation)

 

Asset NBV at Retirement:

  • Area 01 NBV: PKR 1,001,798

  • Area 02 NBV: PKR 8,519

  • Total NBV: PKR 1,010,317

Since NBV exceeds sale value, system correctly calculates overall loss of PKR 515,488.

Observation:

While posting F-92, system splits sales proceeds into:

  • Asset Sale Clearing account

  • Gain on Disposal account

At the same time, system also posts Loss on Disposal entries, resulting in journal entry showing both gain and loss lines.

Question:

Is it standard SAP FI-AA behavior that F-92 postings can show both gain and loss lines due to valuation layer or depreciation area split, even in full retirement?

Any guidance or reference would be appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Compensation Statements in People Profile
entry.content:
If you enable to People Profile block for the statements, the RBP Permissions will dictate visibility in conjunction with the visibility rules within the template.   Meaning if you give a manager permissions to the statements in RBP and enable the block in profile, they will only see the statements if the permissions are granted as default or specifically granted for that compensation template. 

I believe the other permission will dictate if you can see the statement links in the completed form or in executive review.   This may need a RBP expert to fully answer the question.

entry.title: Re: C_TADM_23 : SAP Certified S/4HANA System Administrato...
entry.content:
Hi @DieterLorenz,

The traditional C_TADM content no longer reflects how cloud operations work today — so instead of replacing it with a one‑to‑one successor, SAP is guiding learners toward several more role‑aligned paths.

The best choice depends on where you want to grow:

  • E_S4CPE SAP Certified Specialist - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 
    Designed for consultants but very relevant for S/4HANA Cloud admins, since cloud administration shares many responsibilities with consulting in the S/4C environment.
  • C_DBADM SAP Certified Associate - Database Administrator - SAP HANA 
    Ideal if you want to focus on database administration in cloud landscapes.
  • C_ADBTP SAP Certified - SAP BTP Administrator 
    Perfect for learners moving toward SAP BTP administration, a rapidly growing cloud skill area.
Not an exam, but highly recommended
If you’re transitioning into cloud administration, make sure to complete the learning journey: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Interaction with SAP for Administrations This guided path shows you exactly how to work with the SAP Enterprise Cloud Services team — including how to submit requests and handle activities that admins previously performed themselves. Completing it earns you a Record of Achievement (ROA) and sets you up for success in cloud‑based operations.

Kind regards,
Margit

entry.title: SAP TM Report /SCMTMS/CONDITION_CC- Check and Repair consistency of conditions
entry.content:
SAP TM Report /SCMTMS/CONDITION_CC- Check and Repair consistency of conditions.

Introduction

SAP Transportation Management (TM) relies heavily on conditions to determine pricing, costs, and various business rules throughout transportation processes. However, maintaining consistency across complex transportation scenarios can be challenging, especially in large-scale implementations where data integrity issues may arise over time.

SAP Note 3533245 introduces a powerful Check & Correct Tool specifically designed to address condition-related inconsistencies in SAP TM systems. This tool, accessible via report /SCMTMS/CONDITION_CC, provides automated validation and correction capabilities for condition data, helping transportation managers maintain optimal system performance and data quality.

This blog is intended for SAP TM consultants, system administrators, and functional experts who need to ensure condition data integrity in their transportation management processes.

Understanding the Problem

Transportation management systems handle vast amounts of condition data that determine freight costs, surcharges, discounts, and other pricing elements. Common condition-related issues in SAP TM include:

Data Inconsistencies: Over time, condition records may become inconsistent due to system updates, data migrations, or manual maintenance activities. These inconsistencies can lead to incorrect pricing calculations and transportation cost determinations.

Performance Impact: Inconsistent condition data can significantly impact system performance, particularly during freight unit processing, transportation planning, and cost calculation processes. Users may experience slower response times and processing delays.

Manual Correction Challenges: Identifying and correcting condition inconsistencies manually is time-consuming and error-prone, especially in systems with thousands of condition records across multiple transportation scenarios.

SAP Note 3533245 Overview

SAP Note 3533245 addresses the critical need for automated condition validation and correction in SAP TM environments . The note provides a comprehensive solution for identifying and resolving condition-related inconsistencies that may occur during system operations.

Key Improvements: The note introduces the /SCMTMS/CONDITION_CC report, which offers systematic checking and correction capabilities for condition data integrity. This tool helps maintain optimal system performance by ensuring condition records remain consistent and accurate.

System Coverage: The correction applies to various SAP TM versions and provides essential functionality for maintaining condition data quality across different transportation management scenarios.

The Check & Correct Tool (/SCMTMS/CONDITION_CC)

What is the Tool?

The /SCMTMS/CONDITION_CC report is a specialized diagnostic and correction tool designed to validate condition data integrity within SAP TM systems . This tool integrates seamlessly with the SAP TM condition framework, providing comprehensive analysis and automated correction capabilities for condition-related inconsistencies.

The tool operates by systematically examining condition records, identifying potential issues, and offering correction options to resolve detected problems. It serves as both a diagnostic instrument and a corrective solution for maintaining optimal condition data quality.

Key Features

Comprehensive Condition Validation: The tool performs thorough validation checks across all condition types and records, identifying inconsistencies, missing data, and potential performance issues that may affect transportation processes.

Automated Correction Capabilities: Beyond identification, the tool provides automated correction functionality, allowing users to resolve detected issues efficiently without manual intervention for routine problems.

Detailed Reporting and Logging: The tool generates comprehensive reports detailing all identified issues, correction actions taken, and system status updates, providing full transparency and audit trails for condition maintenance activities.

How to Access and Use the Tool

Prerequisites

Before using the Check & Correct Tool, ensure you have the necessary system access and authorizations for condition maintenance in SAP TM. Users should possess appropriate authorization objects for transportation management and condition processing.

System Requirements: Verify that SAP Note 3533245 has been properly implemented in your system environment. The tool requires specific system components and may have dependencies on other SAP TM functionalities.

Preparation Steps: It's recommended to perform the tool execution during maintenance windows or low-activity periods to minimize impact on ongoing transportation processes. Consider creating system backups before executing correction functions.

Step-by-Step Usage Guide

  1. Navigation: Access the tool by entering transaction code /SCMTMS/CONDITION_CC in the SAP command field or navigate through the SAP TM menu structure to locate the Check & Correct Tool.
  2. Parameter Selection: Configure the tool parameters based on your specific requirements. This may include selecting condition types, date ranges, or specific transportation document types for analysis.
  3. Execution and Analysis: Execute the tool to perform condition validation checks. Review the generated analysis results to understand identified issues and their potential impact on system performance.
  4. Correction Implementation: Based on the analysis results, implement the recommended corrections using the tool's automated correction capabilities or manual intervention as appropriate.

Enter transaction SE38 or SA38 with the name of report and execute it:

Selecting the condition will show right side bellow the message:

Once execute to repair message show bellow:

Benefits and Use Cases

Business Benefits

Enhanced Data Quality: The tool significantly improves condition data quality by systematically identifying and correcting inconsistencies, leading to more accurate pricing and cost calculations in transportation processes.

Operational Efficiency: Automated validation and correction capabilities reduce manual effort required for condition maintenance, allowing transportation teams to focus on strategic activities rather than data maintenance tasks.

System Performance Optimization: By maintaining consistent condition data, the tool helps optimize system performance, reducing processing times for freight calculations and transportation planning activities.

 Conclusion

The SAP TM Check & Correct Tool for Conditions represents a significant advancement in maintaining condition data integrity within transportation management systems. By providing automated validation and correction capabilities, this tool helps organizations maintain optimal system performance while reducing manual maintenance efforts.

Implementation of SAP Note 3533245 and regular use of the /SCMTMS/CONDITION_CC report should be considered essential components of any comprehensive SAP TM maintenance strategy. The tool's ability to proactively identify and resolve condition inconsistencies makes it invaluable for maintaining system reliability and data quality.

Organizations should prioritize implementing this tool as part of their regular system maintenance routines, ensuring transportation processes continue to operate efficiently with accurate condition data supporting critical business decisions.

Refer:

3533245 - Check & Correct Tool for Conditions - SAP for Me

Hope that can help you.

Thanks, and regards

Rogerio Lima.

entry.title: OData V4 URL Encoding Issue with SAP Web Dispatcher + Proxy (Sales Order Management App)
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

We are facing an issue with OData V4 URL encoding after activating the standard Sales Order Management application in S/4HANA.

Note: An SAP incident has already been created, and in parallel we are reaching out to the community to learn from experts who may have faced a similar issue.

Issue summary

The problem occurs because equal signs (=) in the OData V4 request URL are being URL-encoded (%3D) before the request reaches the S/4HANA ICM.

In the browser, the request URL is correct and the = signs are not encoded:

https://etgwdsp.etgworld.com/sap/opu/odata4/sap/c_salesordermanage_srv/srvd_f4/sap/c_orgdivisionvaluehelp/0001;ps='srvd-c_salesordermanage_sd-0001';va='com.sap.gateway.srvd.c_salesordermanage_sd.v0001.ae-c_salesordermanage.createwithsalesordertype.organizationdivision.SalesOrderManageType.X'/$metadata?sap-language=EN

However, when the request reaches the ICM, the raw HTTP request shows that:

  • = is encoded as %3D

  • %27 is converted back to '

Example from ICM level 3 trace:

GET /sap/opu/odata4/.../0001;ps%3D'srvd-c_salesordermanage_sd-0001';va%3D'com.sap.gateway.srvd.c_salesordermanage_sd.v0001.ae-c_salesordermanage.createwithsalesordertype.organizationdivision.SalesOrderManageType.X'/$metadata?sap-language=EN

The SAP Gateway expects the “=” characters to remain unencoded, and because of this encoding, the request fails.

Landscape and behavior

Middleware involved

  • SAP Web Dispatcher

  • Corporate HTTP Proxy

Observed behavior

  1. All apps work when the proxy is bypassed (Web Dispatcher active)

  2. All apps work when the Web Dispatcher is bypassed (proxy active)

  3. The issue occurs only when both proxy and Web Dispatcher are active

This strongly indicates that the URL is being modified due to an interaction between the proxy and Web Dispatcher.

Troubleshooting performed

  • All required roles, authorizations, services, and ICF nodes are active

  • Web Dispatcher profile parameters adjusted to prevent URL encoding

  • Web Dispatcher mod file changes attempted for OData V4 handling
    → Issue still persists

As per internal network team analysis:

  • A change may be required in the Web Dispatcher index file

  • Neither the network team nor BASIS team has access to modify it

  • Only SAP can make changes at this level

Questions to the community

  • Has anyone experienced a similar OData V4 URL encoding issue with Web Dispatcher + proxy?

  • Is this a known limitation or defect in SAP Web Dispatcher for OData V4?

  • Are there any supported parameters, SAP Notes, or workarounds to prevent encoding of = in the URL?

  • Should this be handled via ICM settings, Web Dispatcher configuration, or proxy rules?

  • Is there any recommended architectural workaround until SAP provides a fix?

Any guidance, experiences, or references would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance for your support.

Varsha J S

 

#SAP #S4HANA #ODataV4 #SAPGateway #SAPWebDispatcher #ICM #SAPBasis #SAPFiori #SAPCommunity
@SAP @SAPCommunity @SAPSupport

entry.title: S/4HANA 2025: gCTS 'Synchronous Push' issue with GitLab repository
entry.content:
Hi,

I configured gCTS with a GitLab repository in an SAP S/4HANA 2025 environment. While commits work correctly upon releasing the transports, I have encountered an issue with the 'Push Objects' functionality during synchronous execution.

Debugging the point of failure reveals that the exception occurs because the system cannot identify the API user.

Comparing this method with gCTS in S/4HANA 2023, I noticed that the API object is only instantiated when using GitHub. Since I am using GitLab, the code to retrieve the API object is bypassed, which is why 'Synchronous Push Object' works correctly in that version.

Is anyone else experiencing this error using gCTS on S/4HANA 2025 + GitLab during a 'Synchronous Push Object'? If you're using gCTS on S/4HANA 2025 +GitHub instead, I’d appreciate your feedback to confirm if the functionality works in that setup.

Thank you,
Alfonso.

entry.title: Re: How to Test OAuth-Enabled Inbound Communication in SAP Using an API Client
entry.content:
No - the IAS is not involved.
Bruno is the OAuth Client and the ABAP tenant is the (combined) OAuth Authorization Server & Resource Server.

In your Communication Arrangement (which you are trying to test with Bruno) you should find "OAuth 2.0 Details" (in the "Inbound Communication" section). When you click on that a new window opens which displays the Client ID, Token Endpoint URL, Authorization Endpoint URL and a list of OAuth Scope IDs (one per exposed service).
Mark one of them an then click on "Granted by Business Catalogs".  Your user needs to be assigned to one of them.
You might have to repeat this step for the other Scopes listed on the UI.

entry.title: Skills Framework 101
entry.content:
Skills Framework 101 - Practical Guide

This blog will help you design a skills framework that is usable (not a giant spreadsheet nobody maintains).

1) What a “Skills Framework” really is

A skills framework is a structured way to define:

  • Skills (what people can do),
  • Proficiency levels (how well they can do it),
  • Roles (who needs which skills), and
  • How skills are assessed & maintained over time.

Simple definition: Skills + proficiency + role mapping + governance.

2) The 5 building blocks (I tried to keep it simple)

2.1 Skills Library

  • A list of approved skills (e.g., Stakeholder Management, SQL, Coaching, Forecasting).
  • Rule: One skill = one meaning (avoid duplicates like Stakeholder Mgmt vs Stakeholder Management).
  • Naming standard: Keep names short and clear. Add a one-line definition for every skill.

2.2 Proficiency Scale (how you measure skill strength)

Use a 4–5 level scale max. Example:

  • 1 – Basic: Understands concepts, needs guidance
  • 2 – Working: Can apply independently for standard cases
  • 3 – Advanced: Handles complex cases, improves methods, mentors others
  • 4 – Expert: Sets standards, recognized specialist

Tip: Keep definitions short and observable. Long definitions reduce adoption.

2.3 Role-to-Skills Mapping (what matters for each role)

  • For each role/job family, define top 8–15 skills (not 60).
  • For each skill, set a target proficiency (the minimum expectation).
  • Add priority: Must-have vs Good-to-have.

2.4 Assessment Method (how you capture skill levels)

Pick one starting method and expand later:

  • Self-assessment (fast, good for baseline)
  • Manager validation (improves trust)
  • Evidence-based (certifications, projects, tests) for critical skills later

2.5 Governance (who owns it & how it stays clean)

  • Owner: Skills COE / Talent / HRIT
  • Change control: Request → Review → Approve → Publish
  • Monthly hygiene: merge duplicates, retire old skills, update definitions

3) The minimum viable (MVP) skills framework

If you want a framework that actually gets adopted, start with:

  • 20–60 core skills across your first 2 job families
  • 4-level proficiency scale
  • 8–12 skills per role (with target proficiency)
  • Self-assessment + manager validation for Must-have skills

Why this works: It’s small enough to maintain and big enough to deliver value quickly.

4) Step-by-step: how to build it (practical approach)

Step 1: Decide your “skills scope”

  • Start with 1–2 job families (pilot).
  • Pick roles with clear outcomes (e.g., AE, FP&A Analyst).

Step 2: Build a clean skills list

  • Collect from: job descriptions, role profiles, top performer interviews, enablement content.
  • Normalize names (one naming standard), add short definitions.
  • Remove duplicates and overly broad skills (Communication alone is too generic).

Step 3: Define proficiency levels

  • Keep it consistent across all skills.
  • Ensure each level is observable (what you can see in actions and outputs).

Step 4: Map skills to roles

  • For each role, define:
    • Must-have skills (core)
    • Good-to-have skills (differentiators)
    • Target proficiency for each

Step 5: Decide how you will capture skills

  • Start with self-assessment to get coverage.
  • Add manager validation for Must-have skills (this improves data trust).
  • Later: connect skills to learning, gigs, internal opportunities.

Step 6: Put governance in place

  • Define who can create new skills (keep it controlled).
  • Define review cadence (monthly/quarterly).
  • Define retirement rules for unused skills.

5) Complete examples (copy/paste starters): Sales + Finance

This section gives two full examples that new organizations can adopt immediately: Job Family → Role → Skills library → Proficiency scale → Targets → Assessment → Governance.

5A) Example 1 - Job Family: Sales

5A.1 Job Family

  • Job Family: Sales
  • Purpose: Acquire and grow revenue through pipeline creation, closing, and account expansion
  • Common Roles: SDR/BDR, Account Executive (AE), Account Manager (AM), Sales Manager

5A.2 Role

  • Role Name: Account Executive (AE) - Mid Market
  • Role Summary: Owns pipeline from discovery to close; manages forecast; closes new business
  • Typical Outcomes: Qualified pipeline, closed-won deals, accurate forecast, win rate improvement

5A.3 Proficiency scale (4 levels)

LevelLabelObservable behavior
1BasicUnderstands concepts; follows playbooks with guidance
2WorkingRuns standard cycles independently; consistent results in typical cases
3AdvancedHandles complex deals; adapts approach; mentors peers
4ExpertSets standards; drives strategy; recognized authority

5A.4 Skills library (starter set for Sales)

SkillTypeShort definitionExample evidence
Discovery & QualificationFunctionalIdentifies pain, value, stakeholders, and fitClean discovery notes; clear problem + impact
Value SellingFunctionalLinks solution to measurable outcomesROI story; quantified value hypothesis
NegotiationFunctionalNegotiates terms while protecting valueTrade-offs, reduced discounting, deal desk outcomes
Pipeline ManagementFunctionalCreates and progresses opportunities through stagesStage hygiene; consistent pipeline coverage
ForecastingFunctionalProduces reliable forecast using evidence signalsForecast accuracy vs actuals
Stakeholder ManagementBehavioralBuilds influence across champions, blockers, execsMulti-threading map; exec alignment
CRM DisciplineTechnicalMaintains accurate CRM activity and stage criteriaComplete CRM records; audit-ready
Competitive PositioningFunctionalDifferentiates credibly against competitorsWin/loss notes; talk tracks

5A.5 Role → skill targets (small plus usable)

RoleSkillPriorityTarget levelWhat “good” looks like
AE – Mid MarketDiscovery & QualificationMust-have3Consistent qualification; strong close plans; crisp next steps
AE – Mid MarketValue SellingMust-have3Quantifies impact; ties to customer KPIs
AE – Mid MarketPipeline ManagementMust-have3Healthy pipeline; stage criteria met; proactive risk mgmt
AE – Mid MarketNegotiationMust-have2Protects value; avoids discount traps; uses trade-offs
AE – Mid MarketForecastingMust-have2Evidence-backed calls; fewer surprises
AE – Mid MarketStakeholder ManagementMust-have2Multi-threading; sponsor identified; blockers managed
AE – Mid MarketCRM DisciplineMust-have2Clean notes; activity maps to stage progress
AE – Mid MarketCompetitive PositioningGood-to-have2Clear differentiation; handles objections

5A.6 Assessment method

  • Baseline: Self-assessment on all mapped skills
  • Trust layer: Manager validation on Must-have skills only
  • Evidence (later): win rate, forecast accuracy, call coaching, certifications

5A.7 Governance

  • Owner: Sales Enablement + HR Talent (Skills Steward group)
  • Change control: request intake weekly; publish monthly
  • Hygiene: deduplicate quarterly; retire unused annually

5B) Example 2 - Job Family: Finance

5B.1 Job Family

  • Job Family: Finance
  • Purpose: Governance, accurate reporting, compliance, and decision support
  • Common Roles: Financial Analyst, FP&A Analyst, Accountant, Finance Business Partner (FBP), Controller

5B.2 Role

  • Role Name: FP&A Analyst
  • Role Summary: Owns budget/forecast cycles, variance analysis, and performance insights
  • Typical Outcomes: forecast accuracy, timely insights, stakeholder decisions

5B.3 Proficiency scale ( An example of 4 levels)

LevelLabelObservable behavior
1BasicUnderstands concepts; needs templates and guidance
2WorkingRuns standard analyses independently; reliable outputs
3AdvancedHandles ambiguity; builds models; influences decisions
4ExpertSets planning standards; shapes strategy; recognized authority

5B.4 Skills library (starter set for Finance)

SkillTypeShort definitionExample evidence
Budgeting & ForecastingFunctionalBuilds budgets/forecasts and runs planning cyclesCycle ownership; forecast submissions
Variance AnalysisFunctionalExplains deviations vs plan and prior periodsMonthly variance pack; drivers identified
Financial ModelingTechnicalBuilds models for scenarios and business casesSensitivity analysis; scenario model
Management ReportingFunctionalCreates exec-ready insights and KPI packsBoard/leadership pack; KPI dashboard
Cost ManagementFunctionalTracks and optimizes cost driversCost-to-serve analysis; savings tracking
Accounting BasicsFunctionalUnderstands GL, accruals, revenue/expense principlesClose support; accrual logic
Compliance & Controls AwarenessBehavioralUnderstands audit/control expectationsControl checklist; audit responses
Business PartneringBehavioralInfluences stakeholders with recommendationsDecision memos; stakeholder alignment

5B.5 Role → skill targets

RoleSkillPriorityTarget levelWhat “good” looks like
FP&A AnalystBudgeting & ForecastingMust-have3Owns cycle; improves forecast accuracy; clear assumptions
FP&A AnalystVariance AnalysisMust-have3Finds drivers fast; explains clearly to non-finance
FP&A AnalystFinancial ModelingMust-have2Models with assumptions, sensitivity, and sanity checks
FP&A AnalystManagement ReportingMust-have2Exec-ready storytelling with KPIs and actions
FP&A AnalystBusiness PartneringMust-have2Influences decisions with recommendations
FP&A AnalystAccounting BasicsGood-to-have2Understands accruals and close impacts
FP&A AnalystCompliance & Controls AwarenessGood-to-have1Understands control boundaries; escalates risks early

5B.6 Assessment method

  • Baseline: Self-assessment
  • Validation: Manager validation on Budgeting/Forecasting + Variance Analysis
  • Evidence (later): forecast accuracy trend, cycle timeliness, model quality reviews

5B.7 Governance

  • Owner: Finance COE (or Controller org) + HR Talent (Skills Steward group)
  • Change control: monthly review board for new skills + mapping changes

6) What to do in SAP SuccessFactors (so this becomes “LIVE”)

This is the execution path to operationalize Sales + Finance skills in SuccessFactors using TIH (Talent Intelligence Hub) and the People Profile.

6.1 Build the foundation (1–2 weeks)

  • Confirm your model approach: TIH as the system of record for Skills.

TIH Skills Library: centralized list of skills with status, tags, and created type.

  • Create the proficiency scale: Set up the 4-level scale (Beginner → Intermediate → Proficient → Expert).

TIH Rating Scale (Proficiency Level Scale): define proficiency levels and descriptions once, then reuse across skills and attribute types.

  • Create your Skills Library: Start with the Sales + Finance skills tables above.

TIH Skill record: maintain a clean definition, status, tags, and associated proficiency levels to keep the skills library governed and reusable.

6.2 Connect skills to roles/job profiles (1–2 weeks)

  • Job Architecture/Job Profiles: Ensure the roles exist (AE Mid Market, FP&A Analyst) in your job architecture / job profile setup.
  • Map skills to roles: Assign 8–12 skills per role and define Target Proficiency + Priority (Must-have/Good-to-have).
  • Keep it consistent: Same skill name/definition across all roles (avoid duplicates).

Role-based skill mapping (Job Profile Builder): map skills to a role and optionally maintain an expected proficiency level per skill.

6.3 Expose skills to employees and managers (1 week)

  • People Profile: Enable the Skills (TIH) section so employees can view skills.

Latest People Profile (Spotlight): Skills and Competencies are surfaced as cards for quick visibility and self-branding.

  • Permissions (RBP):
    • Employees: view/edit own skills
    • Managers: view team skills; validate/confirm where you allow
    • Skills Stewards/Admin: create/edit skills and mappings

6.4 Launch a pilot (2–4 weeks)

  • Pilot scope: Sales + Finance only (start with these two roles).
  • Campaign 1: Self-assessment on all mapped skills (simple guidance; avoid long instructions).
  • Campaign 2: Manager validation only on Must-have skills (reduces manager load and increases trust).

6.5 Make it useful fast

  • Gap reporting: Role targets vs current proficiency distribution by skill.
  • Learning linkage: Link learning items to top gap skills.
    • Sales examples: discovery, value selling, negotiation, forecasting
    • Finance examples: forecasting, modeling, variance analysis, reporting
  • Mobility readiness (optional later): identify near-ready talent based on skills targets.

6.6 Governance inside SuccessFactors - keep the data clean

  • Create a “Skills Steward” permission group: only this group can create/edit skills and role mappings.
  • Monthly library hygiene: duplicates, unused skills, naming conflicts.
  • Quarterly mapping review: refresh targets when job roles change (sales motions, finance processes, tool changes).
  • Retirement rule: retire skills unused for 12 months (or mark obsolete and map to an alternative).

7) Common mistakes (and how to avoid them)

  • Too many skills: If a role has 40 skills, nobody will complete it → keep 8–12 to start.
  • Vague skill names: Communication is too broad → specify “Executive Communication” or “Customer Communication”.
  • No governance: Without ownership, duplicates explode.
  • No use case: If skills don’t connect to hiring, learning, mobility, or performance, they die.
  • No manager involvement: Self-assessment alone often becomes optimistic data. Validate Must-haves.

Quick wins you can launch in 2–4 weeks

  • Sales + Finance pilot with 2 roles and 8–12 skills each
  • Self-assessment campaign (baseline coverage)
  • Manager validation for Must-have skills only
  • Skills gap dashboard (target vs current distribution)
  • Learning linkage for top 3 gaps per role

What's next

  • Pick your pilot population (which Sales org + which Finance org).
  • Confirm the final 8–12 skills per role and lock naming/definitions.
  • Implement the proficiency scale and skills library inSAP SuccessFactors (TIH).
  • Map skills to roles/job profiles with targets and priorities.
  • Run the pilot assessment + manager validation and publish gap insights.
  • Scale to more roles after the first 30–60 days.

Keywords: Skills Framework, Skills Taxonomy, Proficiency, Role Mapping, Governance, Talent Intelligence Hub, Skills-Based Organization, SAP SuccessFactors, Growth Portfolio

entry.title: Re: My highlights of the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release
entry.content:
Dear Sunny, 

my name is Daniel, and I am leading the Early Adopter Care Program for SAP-managed Integration. 

Thank you for your additional questions and I am happy to provide more clarity.

1. For the first phase of the Early Adopter Program we start with greenfield implementations. Meaning both systems SAP SuccessFactors EC and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition have to be provisioned from the start. Our ambition is to extend this in the next phase of the program to customers, who adopt first one Product (e.g. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition) and after their go-live extend their landscape with additional capabilities (e.g. SAP SuccessFactors EC). 

2. Extensibility is a critical piece for sap-managed Integration. Custom field(s) are supported. 

3. Let me provide the short answer: SAP is responsible for the technical integration to ensure stable operations. CALM is used to communicate issues to the customer. Business related issues have to be resolved by the customer. My colleague @tilo_eggeling will provide additional context, as this is a comprehensive topic. 

Thanks,

Daniel

entry.title: Leveraging Joule Custom Skills in S/4HANA PCE: A Clean Core Approach for Sourcing & Procurement
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community!

As a consultant focused on RISE with SAP and Clean Core strategies, I’ve been exploring how Joule (SAP’s Generative AI) can transform the user experience within S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (PCE), specifically for Sourcing & Procurement.

Integrating a custom skill developed in SAP Build with the Fiori Launchpad (S/4HANA 2025) is more than just a technical milestone; it is a strategic shift. For those of us managing large-scale environments, this represents a powerful way to handle extensibility Side-by-Side on SAP BTP, keeping the core stable and upgrade-ready.

Key Insights from my recent implementation:

  1. Enhancing Procurement Efficiency: By leveraging custom skills, we can empower users to query complex contract details or PR/PO statuses through natural language, significantly reducing the volume of L1/L2 support tickets.

  2. Clean Core Alignment: Using SAP Build Actions to consume OData APIs ensures that we are following SAP Best Practices, avoiding legacy "Z" developments inside the S/4HANA core.

  3. The SAP Activate Connection: During the Run phase, Joule acts as a continuous enabler, helping users navigate business processes without leaving the Fiori environment.

I’d love to hear your thoughts: For those already working with Joule in S/4HANA 2025 PCE, how are you handling the governance of these custom skills? Are you prioritizing "Read-only" informational skills, or are you moving towards transactional "Write" scenarios (e.g., creating Purchase Requisitions) in production?

Looking forward to exchanging experiences on this AI journey!

#RISEwithSAP #S4HANA #SAPMM #CleanCore #Joule #SAPBuild #BTP #SourcingAndProcurement

entry.title: Re: Does 1K2 Revenue Recognition work with Intercompany S...
entry.content:
Dear @JavierIbanez,

In Public Cloud scope item 1K2 Event-Based Revenue Recognition Sell from Stock is not supported for the classical intercompany sales from stock scenario which is what 1HO represents

SAP explicitly documents this limitation:

  • SAP Note 3045884 states that event-based revenue recognition for classical intercompany sales from stock is not supported no RA key is assigned to the sales order items, so no EBRR postings are generated. It also states that EBRR supports Advanced Intercompany Sales (5D2)

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3045884

So, NO you aren’t missing configuration this is a scope/functional limitation not a missing CBC setting. If you’re on 1HO the observed behavior no RA key / no EBRR postings is expected per SAP

What to do instead:

Move from 1HO -> 5D2 (Advanced Intercompany Sales) if you need event-based revenue recognition in intercompany sales. SAP positions 5D2 as the redesigned process and explicitly notes it’s embedded into areas including revenue recognition

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/implementing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-sales-advanced-business-processes/executing-and-running-advanced-intercompany-sales-5d2-

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: 自定义业务对象删除失败
entry.content:
Good day @weiding 

Thank you for your question.

From your screenshots, the error about the custom business object YY1_CES even though dependency details look empty can be caused by a few factors:

  • The CBO was already transported (either to test or production). The public cloud does not allow you to delete transported CBOs. This KBA 2565500 addresses it.
  • Only certain CBO types are deletable when you meet the prerequisites. An example would be custom modifier type and not transported constraints.
  • In some cases, this can happen due to technical lock objects. SAP might have to check this for you. See KBA 3657645 for more insights.

The reason why no dependency shown can still fail is, the extensibility inventory screen may not show a business dependency, but deletion can still be blocked by lifecycle rules such as transport history or technical runtime state. See KBA 2565500 for more details.

I suggest you:

  • Check whether YY1_CES was ever transported to another system.
  • Check CBO status or history.
  • If it is transported, do not delete. Rather hide it.
  • If it is not transported and still failing, I suggest you open an incident with SAP.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

 

 

 

感谢您的提问。
根据您提供的截图，关于自定义业务对象YY1_CES的错误提示（即使依赖关系详情显示为空）可能由以下因素导致：
• 该CBO已被传输（至测试环境或生产环境）

。公共云不允许删除已传输的CBO，此问题已在知识库文章2565500中说明。
• 仅特定类型的CBO在满足前提条件时可删除，例如自定义修饰符类型，而非已传输的约束条件。
• 某些情况下可能由技术锁定对象导致。SAP可能需要为您检查此问题，详见KBA 3657645获取更多信息。
即使未显示依赖关系仍可能失败的原因在于：可扩展性库存界面可能未显示业务依赖关系，但删除操作仍可能因生命周期规则（如传输历史或技术运行时状态）而受阻。详情参见KBA 2565500。
建议您：
• 检查 YY1_CES 是否曾传输至其他系统
• 核查 CBO 状态或历史记录
• 若已传输，请勿删除，改为隐藏
• 若未传输仍失败，建议向 SAP 提交事件
如有疑问请随时告知。
此致
敬礼
Chris

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

“SAP GenAI was used to help generate this content”

entry.title: SAP HCM OM HRMD_A idoc not updating Relationships in the target system
entry.content:
Hi All,

Any idea about the below issue....

SAP HCM OM.

we have Positions reporting structure as A<-B<-C<-D in system X. D is changed to report to A instead of C. This change has to interface through Idoc from SAP System X to SAP system Z. 

X system and Z system had the same configuration and reporting structure before this relationship change. 

The normal HRMD_A Message type and HRMD_A09 Idoc type interface is creating Idoc in X system and sending to Z system. In Z system Idoc is processed with 53 status.but in System Z its not updating the relationship change from D->A.

Debugged the FM IDOC_INPUT_HRMD for the inbound Idoc in Z System and the reason is an entry for 002 is missing in the below path IMG->Personal Management ->Organizational Management -> Basic Settings->Data Model Enhancement->Relationship Maintenance -> Maintain Relationships->Additional Data on Relationships. Table T77AR. Is this correct ?

When Checked in the table T77AR, SAP has not provided any entry for the 002 relationship. Sending OM data through HRMD_A Idoc should be already running in sevaral clients. Why SAP has not maintained an entry in this T77AR table ? 

Is the reason of entry missing in T77AR is correct or any other issue is preventing the relationship update in system Z ?

entry.title: CBC project stuck
entry.content:
Hi , 

I made a change to the org structure and it has been stuck on 60% deployed for over two hour now .

Please the lock  can be release .

Regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: CBC project stuck
entry.content:
Hi!

If you experience the Deploy Scope and Organizational Structure activity stopping in 60% during deployment, this is generally expected behavior.

During the initial deployment, SAP Central Business Configuration prepares, deploys, and activates the selected scope and organizational content in the connected SAP S/4HANA Cloud tenant. As part of this process, once the activity is executed, the workspace is temporarily locked to avoid inconsistencies while deployment and activation are finalized in the backend. This does not indicate an error by itself.

After the deployment is completed successfully, the workspace automatically moves to the Product-Specific Configuration phase. When this happens, you can proceed with your configuration activities as usual.

If no error messages are shown and the workspace advances to Product-Specific Configuration, this confirms that the deployment and activation finished successfully.

For more details about this behavior, please refer to: 3288076 - Deploy Scope and Organizational Structure stuck at 60% progress - SAP for Me. 

Best Regards,

entry.title: Re: Email Notification for PO approvers in sap s4hana pub...
entry.content:
Dear @GOGUL,

What you describe PO appears in My Inbox but no email and even Monitor Email Transmission shows Sent yet nothing arrives matches a deliverability issue on the email infrastructure side, not the workflow logic

In Public Cloud the most common root cause for Status = Sent but not received is:

  • SPF record missing for the sender domain and/or
  • DKIM not set up (mandatory) for the sender domain used in outbound emails

SAP Notes below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003274509

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003223182

Double check:

1. First verify the actual sender address SAP is using

In Monitor Email Transmission open one of the Sent entries and check Sender

Now ensure these 3 points in CBC settings are correct in the same tenant where you are testing:

101311 Define Default Email Sender Address for Email Outbound

  • Maintain a real sender like no-reply@yourcompany.com or your agreed sender mailbox
  • If this is empty/mis-set, SAP may use a different technical sender than you expect

101313 Define Allowed Email Sender Domains for Email Outbound

  • Must allow the domain of the sender from A

101312 Define Allowed Email Receiver Domains for Email Outbound

  • Must allow all recipient domains used in the workflow/test
  • Important, if a workflow notification has multiple recipients and one recipient domain isn’t allowed, SAP may block sending for the whole notification setup

SAP Note below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003112088

Also note these outbound-email domain settings are not transportable and must be maintained per tenant/system

SAP Note below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003556392

2. If status is Sent but no one receives it

SAP explicitly states DKIM setup is mandatory to send emails after using/changing a custom sender domain

SAP Note below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003223182

And SAP confirms Sent but not received is caused by missing SPF and/or DKIM

SAP Note below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003274509

3. If still missing email

SAP has a specific guidance

SAP Note below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003475256

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: BTE 1820 causing dump when calling WLS Screening REST API
entry.content:
Hello ,

We have a requirement to block or allow payments during the F110 payment proposal run based on the response from the SAP Watch List Screening (WLS) REST API.

To achieve this, we implemented BTE 00001820, where we: Call the WLS REST API (send vendor + bank)  and Process the response to update the zlspr

Functionally, the logic works as expected when i debug the loop i get the items and each time the api runs and return the result . However, after finishing the loop , we consistently get the following runtime dump:

Runtime Error: DBSQL_INVALID_CURSOR

Exception: CX_SY_OPEN_SQL_DB

Short Text: Cursor already closed or not open yet

Program: SAPF110S

FORM: BSIK_SELEKTION_2

The dump only occurs when the REST API call is executed. If the REST call block is commented out, the dump no longer occurs. This suggests that calling the REST API inside BTE 1820 is interfering with open database cursors during the F110 processing.

Is there another BTE that is better suited for implementing this logic and setting the payment block (ZLSPR) based on screening results i did some search but could't find the suitable one ?

Alternatively, is there a safe way to keep this implementation in BTE 1820 without causing the DBSQL_INVALID_CURSOR dump?

Any guidance or  recommendations would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Send email notification when an existing Termination...
entry.content:
HI @lornanapper ,

When you perform a correction from history, the workflow will not be triggered, as workflows are not supported for historical edits. One way to achieve this is to create a custom field in Job Info that captures the termination date through a business rule. You can then configure an Alert Notification based on this new custom field. The field visibility can be restricted to Admins. Please let me know if this works.

Regards,

Vijai

entry.title: Re: Update custom SuccessFactors MDF field once workflow...

@YABAPER

You can view the record in SF, but the question is whether the data is being sent via API before the approval is completed. Ideally, if ‘Pending Data’ is set to Yes, the API should only pick it up after the record is approved. You can also try checking the Record Status field (MDF Record Status) — I think it should show ‘P’ when the approval is still pending.

entry.title: Consuming Data Products in SAP HANA Cloud via SAP Business Application Studio/SAP Build Code
entry.content:
This blog post guides you through the process of consuming SAP HANA Cloud Data Products within SAP Business Application Studio (BAS). We will cover everything from the initial SQL security configurations to creating the final Calculation View.

Once a Data Product is installed in your SAP HANA Cloud instance, it exists as virtual tables in a specialized schema. To use it in a Cloud Application Programming (CAP) or HANA Native project in BAS, you must access the virtual tables from your HDI container.

Before you begin this blog, ensure you have followed the initial setup steps to locate and install your data product. I highly recommend completing this guide written by my colleague (Dan Van Leeuwen) : Access and Query a Data Product in SAP HANA Cloud.

Step 1: Assign the roles & privileges (SQL)

To ensure the technical users (HDI Container users) have permission to see the Data Product, We use a Role-Based approach to manage these permissions.

1. Create a dedicated role: This role will act as a container for all permissions related to your Purchase Order data.

CREATE ROLE VIRTUAL_TABLE_ON_PURCHASE_ORDER_OO;
CREATE ROLE VIRTUAL_TABLE_ON_PURCHASE_ORDER_RT;

2. Grant Select with Grant Option: This is crucial. The WITH GRANT OPTION allows the HDI Container user to share these privileges further (e.g., to the end-user reporting tools).

GRANT SELECT ON "_SAP_DATAPRODUCT_sap_s4com_dataProduct_PurchaseOrder_v1_4a6dc5d7-7af5-4b74-8ac7-b9ed0d1e6e95"."_SAP_DATAPRODUCT_57af9989-cf07-4a75-bc36-fddb382b4020_purchaseorder.PurchaseOrder"
TO VIRTUAL_TABLE_ON_PURCHASE_ORDER_OO WITH GRANT OPTION;

GRANT SELECT ON "_SAP_DATAPRODUCT_sap_s4com_dataProduct_PurchaseOrder_v1_4a6dc5d7-7af5-4b74-8ac7-b9ed0d1e6e95"."_SAP_DATAPRODUCT_57af9989-cf07-4a75-bc36-fddb382b4020_purchaseorder.PurchaseOrder"
TO VIRTUAL_TABLE_ON_PURCHASE_ORDER_RT WITH GRANT OPTION;

3. Grants SELECT to the objects.

Spoiler
Granting these roles globally to BAS HDI container object owners and runtime users means that all HDI containers and therefore all BAS developers will automatically gain access to the virtual tables. If your intention is universal access, this approach avoids granting roles individually. However, be aware that it effectively broadens access scope system-wide, which may have governance, security, or compliance implications if more restricted access was intended.
GRANT SELECT ON SCHEMA "_SAP_DATAPRODUCT_sap_s4com_dataProduct_PurchaseOrder_v1_4a6dc5d7-7af5-4b74-8ac7-b9ed0d1e6e95" to VIRTUAL_TABLE_ON_PURCHASE_ORDER_OO WITH GRANT OPTION;
 
GRANT SELECT ON SCHEMA  "_SAP_DATAPRODUCT_sap_s4com_dataProduct_PurchaseOrder_v1_4a6dc5d7-7af5-4b74-8ac7-b9ed0d1e6e95" to VIRTUAL_TABLE_ON_PURCHASE_ORDER_RT;

Step 2: Create your Project in SAP Business Application Studio

1. Open SAP Business Application Studio.

 

2. Create a new SAP HANA Native Application or a CAP Project. In this demo we are showcasing the SAP HANA Native application.  

3. Ensure your project is connected to the correct Cloud Foundry Space and SAP HANA Cloud instance.

4. In the SAP HANA Projects view, click the expand icon to view your project and to check the binding of the HDI Container.

Step 3: Create the Calculation View

  1. Right-click the src folder and select New File -> CV_PurchaseAnalysis.hdbcalculationview.
  2. Choose Data Category as the CUBE and click create.

3.In the aggregation editor, find the data source

4.Click the + sign on the Aggregation node and search for your Purchase Order Table and click on Create Synonym.

5. You will see the below dialog and click finish.

6. Go to the mapping tab, and map the necessary columns to the output column.

 

6. Save and Deploy the file by clicking the "Deploy" icon (the rocket ship) in the SAP HANA Projects panel.

Step 4: Preview the Data

To confirm everything is working:

  1. Right click on the aggregation node on your calculation view and click on Graphical SQL Data Preview.

     

  2. Database Explorer will open in your BAS window. You can drag and drop the required columns and analyze your purchase order details
  3. You should now see the live data flowing from the SAP S/4HANA Data Product directly into your BAS environment.

 

By following these steps, you’ve successfully bridged the gap between raw SAP HANA Cloud Data Products and a flexible development environment in SAP Business Application Studio.

Special thanks to my colleague  jan_zwickel for their support and expert review in making this blog.

Thank you for your time.

entry.title: Re: SPL City field is blank
entry.content: Stopwords work globally on tokenization as I know, not per field. Matching thresholds are profile-wide (one overall score, no field-specific %), and City cannot be ignored when empty, if this causes missed hits, City must be excluded from the SPL profile.

entry.title: Re: Exploring Enhanced Mass Actions and Compensation Forms in Job Scheduler

entry.title: Re: HANA System Database copy scenario
entry.content: You are right to question the wording in Scenario 1. To clarify: xs_worker / xs_standby are not mandatory for system database recovery. They are tenant-related roles. Recovery in Scenario 1 fails not because XS roles are missing, but because the target system topology does not match the system database metadata of the source system. Scenario 2 works because the system database can be recovered on a compatible worker topology, independent of XS roles.

entry.title: Re: The Importance of Primary Finance Settings in SAP Cloud ERP
entry.content:
Hola chloe17

That is a very good question. Let me provide two answers based on each type of system:

1.- CBC for Starter system: 

The Starter System includes SAP demo data for running workshops with the customer. In the CBC system for the Start system, you can choose the ledgers in the Define Scope activity (described in this blog),  and only K4 - Cal. Year 4 Special Periods, as a fiscal-year variant, and USD as the group currency are available.

SAP Help Portal Link Specifying Primary Finance Settings

2.- Development system:

This system does not contain any SAP demo data; it only contains customer data. In the CBC system for the Dev system, you can choose the ledger in the Define Scope activity, select any fiscal year variant, and group currency delivered by SAP.

In both cases, once these activities are marked as completed, the status of the Deploy Scope and Organizational structure is set to Completed, and Deploy is done; it is not possible to go back. 
Also, it is not possible to add ledgers after this.

entry.title: Currency translation question in BPC 10.1 Standard on NW
entry.content:
Hello, 

My question is with regard to a somewhat non-standard requirement; that of loading original TC balances into the BPC model and then translating to Entity local currency plus into other target reporting currencies. In this BPC version it is not allowed to have more than one currency type dimension in either the consolidation or rates model. Hence am using a user-defined dimension for transaction currency code. However, this doesn't appear to be workable - here is the fundamental question on why :

- If I load multiple transaction currency records into the model - say GBP and JPY - and both of these need to be translated to EUR (LC) and subsequently to USD - how can I do this? I can only maintain one rate in the rates model for a rate type and rate entity - so how will BPC know what is the rate from GBP to EUR vs from JPY to EUR? Similarly if I need to translate to USD.

Thanks,

Sri

entry.title: Re: Turning on "Lexicographical" setting for Product masters
entry.content:
Dear @Madhavan_R1,

In Public Cloud the Lexicographical indicator in 102320 Define Output Format of Material Numbers is treated as a system-critical, client-wide switch. SAP blocks changing it as soon as any purely numeric Product/Material IDs have ever existed in that tenant (message BMG265). SAP own guidance is that the indicator can only be set/reset when no numeric material numbers have been used, otherwise existing numeric IDs could become non-interpretable so the supported way to proceed is to archive/remove the numeric materials first, but you probably already know that

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3516603

Can you or SAP just flip it directly in Production:

Practically this is not a supported workaround in Public Cloud:

  • In CBC-driven Public Cloud, product-specific configuration is meant to be performed through the CBC project flow and then deployed/transported. Production is primarily for production-system-specific current settings depending on 2SL/3SL, not for bypassing blocked product master fundamentals

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/configuring-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-manufacturing-execution/introducing-sap-central-business-configuration

  • Even if your Production tenant is empty and would technically allow you to set the indicator there, you would end up with a landscape mismatch DEV/TEST cannot replicate the behavior, meaning you can’t properly test creation/migration/integration with leading-zero product IDs before go-live

Because the block exists explicitly to prevent interpretation issues, SAP Support generally won’t override/bypass it via expert configuration when the system condition is violated numeric materials exist

What I’d do next in your situation:

1. Open an SAP incident under referencing SAP Note 3516603 and explicitly state:

  • DEV/TEST already contain numeric products and are blocked
  • PROD is unused
  • you need a supported path to enable lexicographical handling across the landscape not a one-off PROD-only toggle
  • Waiting for SAP feedback/proposal

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Oil and Gas Industry – SAP Profitability and Performance Management
entry.content:
Dear  Chaitanya,  Thank you for sharing this information.  it provides very helpful context on how SAP PaPM is being applied within the Oil & Gas industry. After reviewing the content, it prompted a question from a downstream perspective, where the operational and valuation requirements differ significantly from upstream scenarios.

In the Oil & Gas content available for SAP PaPM, we understand that the focus is primarily oriented toward upstream processes (for example, cost allocation by wells, fields, activities, and operational drivers).

From a downstream process perspective, it is common to represent:

  • multiple stages of industrial transformation of crude‑derived products,

  • products that may reside in different installations (both company‑owned and external),

  • movements between installations and the associated adjustments,

  • blending operations between different product streams,

  • and the need to reconstruct the cost and value of products across the entire chain.

In addition, these downstream processes typically require complete traceability, both:

  • physical, to follow how the product moves and transforms across installations and units, and

  • economic, to understand how its cost and value are formed at each stage.

In this context, we would appreciate SAP’s guidance on:

1. Whether SAP PaPM is suitable for modelling downstream industrial processes with these characteristics.

2. Whether there is any content or functionality within the SAP ecosystem (for example, within IS‑Oil or S/4HANA) that is specifically oriented toward downstream processes and could serve as a reference.

3. What architectural approach SAP recommends for integrating such a model with S/4HANA’s financial and valuation structures,  particularly regarding cost structures, valuation layers, and obtaining product‑level margins.

4. What architectural approach SAP recommends for representing an end‑to‑end downstream model within S/4HANA, especially in relation to cost and value formation across multiple transformations, movements between installations, and maintaining both physical and economic traceability throughout the chain.                                                                                                                                   

All recommendations are welcome.                                                                                                                                                                   

Best regards,

Gustavo H.

entry.title: Re: Efforts (Planned and Actual)
entry.content:
Hi, manjunathshivappa,

SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation provides some support for agile project and task management. The Task Management app does not allow recording granular dates and times for each user contribution to a Task. Instead, the Tasks are assigned to phases, sprints, and milestones. An individual Task can have its own Planned Completion date and duration. Tasks can be assigned story points and effort points for further assessments and analytics. 

For more information on the SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation reporting capabilities and agile workflows, please refer to this documentation from the SAP Help Portal:

  • SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation - Overview
  • SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation - Analytics 
  • SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation - Tasks - Agile Support
  • SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation - Tasks - Tracking Tasks
  • SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation - Tasks - Gantt Chart View

If the supported functionality does not meet your needs, you can always submit your ideas through the Influence Campaign:

- SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation  https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3501

 - SAP Cloud ALM for Operations https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3525

Best regards,
Paula

entry.title: Re: A-Z Performance Testing for integrations... And why?
entry.content: Thanks for sharing this! I especially agree that performance testing early helps avoid bottlenecks later. Curious — what’s your experience with tools that support both load and integration testing in SAP landscapes?
